doc: Pure-TS port of MPFR's mpfr_buildopt_bfloat16_p: build-time predicate reporting whether libmpfr was compiled with bfloat16 support (MPFR_WANT_BFLOAT16). C returns int (0 or 1) per a preprocessor branch; TS returns boolean. The TS port has no compile-time switches and no native bfloat16 type, so the value is the compile-time constant `false`. The libmpfr-side observable depends on how the system libmpfr was built; the golden_driver emits the TS-expected `false` unconditionally because the TS port does not (and cannot) advertise bfloat16 support. Class 'misc' (1000ms per-case budget). Tag minimums (happy>=20, edge>=30, adversarial>=10, fuzz>=50, mined>=5) are inapplicable to no-arg accessor functions whose input domain is empty — a single happy case is the entire test surface.
divergence_from_c: C returns int (0 or 1) from a preprocessor branch on MPFR_WANT_BFLOAT16; TS returns boolean and is unconditionally `false`. Pure-TS has no bfloat16 IEEE type — the typed-array surface is f32/f64 only — so advertising bfloat16 support would be a lie. If/when a bfloat16 ts library lands and we wire it in, this port flips to `true` and the golden is regenerated. The golden_driver does NOT call libmpfr's mpfr_buildopt_bfloat16_p() for the expected output (its return depends on the system libmpfr build flags) — it hard-codes `false` to match the TS-port contract.
refs: mpfr/src/buildopt.c L45-L53 — the C reference (mpfr_buildopt_bfloat16_p preprocessor branch).
CLAUDE.md Rule 7 — tag minimums (carved out here per the spec.doc note above).
CLAUDE.md Rule 12 — pure-TS port runs on Bun OR Node; no native bfloat16 in either.
